articles.title: Exclusive | Kalshi and Polymarket Are Each Eyeing Roughly $20 Billion Valuations
articles.summary: Two prediction market giants, Kalshi and Polymarket, are both targeting valuations around $20 billion as they gear up for what looks like major funding rounds or exits. These sky-high numbers signal that Wall Street and VCs finally see prediction markets as the next frontier in financial infrastructure, not just a niche betting curiosity.

articles.title: College Hoops Are Big on Kalshi
articles.summary: Kalshi is seeing massive volume on college basketball markets as March Madness approaches and bettors pile into bracket predictions. The surge shows how mainstream prediction markets are becoming for sports betting, potentially opening the door for Kalshi to capture more retail traders who want to bet on everything from tournament outcomes to individual player performances.

articles.title: Free Press Opinion: Prediction markets shouldn’t be so easy to play
articles.summary: The Free Press argues that prediction markets have become too accessible and user-friendly for casual participants. This matters because easier entry typically means more noise from uninformed money, which can actually make markets less efficient at price discovery.

articles.title: Popular prediction markets take heat from lawmakers
articles.summary: Congressional representatives are grilling prediction market platforms over regulatory concerns and potential gambling violations. The scrutiny signals tighter oversight ahead, which could force major platforms to restrict certain markets or face hefty compliance costs that get passed to users.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets A Threat To Horse Racing?
articles.summary: Horse racing traditionalists are reportedly worried that prediction markets could steal betting volume from their centuries-old industry. If political betting becomes mainstream, it could normalize prediction markets enough to make horse racing's parimutuel system look antiquated by comparison.

articles.title: Kalshi sued over ouster of Iran leader prediction market
articles.summary: Kalshi faces legal action after removing a prediction market on Iran's leadership, likely related to content moderation or regulatory pressure. The lawsuit could set important precedent for when platforms can pull politically sensitive markets and what recourse traders have when positions vanish.

articles.title: Kalshi Referral Code ACTION Yields $10 for NBA Predictions, WBC, Any Sport
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering $10 bonuses to new users who sign up with referral code ACTION and make predictions on NBA games, World Baseball Classic, or other sports markets. The promotion signals Kalshi's aggressive push into sports prediction territory, directly competing with traditional sportsbooks by gamifying event outcomes with real money incentives.

articles.title: 2 young billionaires are behind the prediction market boom. They hate each other
articles.summary: The CEOs of America's two biggest prediction market platforms are publicly feuding, turning what should be a growing industry into a personal grudge match. This executive drama matters because when the heads of Kalshi and Polymarket are busy throwing punches instead of building better products, it gives traditional betting companies and regulators more ammunition to dismiss prediction markets as immature toys rather than serious financial instruments.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Are Pricing in Pennies for a $150,000 Bitcoin by March -- Here's Why I Still Wouldn't Bet Against This Crypto Wild Card
articles.summary: Prediction markets are giving Bitcoin almost zero chance of hitting $150,000 by March, despite the crypto's history of defying impossible odds. The massive disconnect between market confidence and Bitcoin's volatility creates exactly the kind of asymmetric betting opportunity that separates smart money from the crowd.

articles.title: Pa. lawmaker proposes regulating prediction markets in Pennsylvania
articles.summary: A Pennsylvania lawmaker wants to create a regulatory framework for prediction markets in the state. This could open the door for legal prediction market operations in Pennsylvania, potentially following the playbook that's made sports betting a massive revenue generator for states.

articles.title: A Technology for a Low-Trust Society
articles.summary: Prediction markets are emerging as the go-to solution for extracting truth from populations that no longer trust traditional institutions or each other. When nobody believes the polls, the pundits, or the official statistics, betting markets become the most reliable signal of what's actually happening in the world.

articles.title: Michigan sues Kalshi, alleges app is skirting state's gambling laws
articles.summary: Michigan's attorney general filed a lawsuit against prediction market Kalshi, claiming the platform violates state gambling regulations by allowing residents to bet on events like elections and economic outcomes. The case could set a precedent for how states regulate prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to either restrict access or face a patchwork of state-by-state legal challenges.
